mission.title: spire: Agent K8S workload attestor support for cgroups v2
mission.description: Agent K8S workload attestor support for cgroups v2. This issue affects 3+ users.

steps.title: Identify spire feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your spire deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=spire
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Agent K8S workload attestor support for cgroups v2

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
* **Version**: 1.5.5+
* **Platform**: K8S with amd64 linux kernel using cgroups v2
* **Subsystem**: Agent k8s workload attestor

Currently k8s workload attestor is extracting podUID and containerUID f

steps.title: Apply the fix for Agent K8S workload attestor support for cgroups v2
steps.description:
**Pull Request check list**

**Affected functionality**
Add support for matching cgroups v2 with systemd driver to docker workloadattestor.

**Description of change**
Apply the `dockerCGroupsRE` to candidate path elements extracted by the `<id>` matcher in the DSL. Related to #4004.
```yaml
hostPID: true
hostNetwork: true
dnsPolicy: ClusterFirstWithHostNet
```

steps.title: Confirm Agent K8S workload attestor support for cgroups v2 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: **Pull Request check list**

**Affected functionality**
Add support for matching cgroups v2 with systemd driver to docker workloadattestor.

**Description of change**
Apply the `dockerCGroupsRE` to candidate path elements extracted by the `<id>` matcher in the DSL. Related to #4004.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
hostPID: true
hostNetwork: true
dnsPolicy: ClusterFirstWithHostNet

k exec -it ds/spire-agent -c spire-agent -- /bin/sh
/opt/spire # cat /proc/302967/cgroup
0::/
/opt/spire # cat /proc/329596/cgroup
0::/../../kubepods-besteffort-podb2dce23b_8b3a_4356_b903_56152d488916.slice/cri-containerd-cf6d9de603ab1f665fc84f88c22f761678fcaae86b98a3936e0b9717740146aa.scope

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
